summary: This is an extremely brief post that serves as a pointer to a longer article on the Stack Overflow blog. Joel highlights the problem of recurring questions on Stack Overflow and directs readers to a post about how the platform handles frequently asked 'long tail' programming questions. The post itself contains essentially no original content beyond a single quoted sentence and a link.
key_points: Recurring questions are a persistent challenge for Q&A platforms like Stack Overflow
The 'long tail' concept applies to programming questions — many niche questions recur across users
Joel used his personal blog as a distribution channel to drive traffic to the Stack Overflow blog
money_quotes: Have you ever noticed how certain questions come up again and again on Stack Overflow sites?
key_insight: Stack Overflow needed a strategy for handling the inevitably recurring programming questions that form the long tail of developer knowledge.
